title: Exposes 7 tools, exceeding the 5 spec capability categories
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools.clj:413 tool-definitions registers search_matches, get_team_stats, search_players, get_standings, get_head_to_head, get_biggest_wins, get_competition_stats
suggestion: None — beyond-spec coverage (biggest_wins, competition_stats) is a positive.

title: competition=brasileirao filter misses pre-2012 seasons in non-standings tools
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools.clj:58 parse-comp-key + data.clj:160 matches-for-competition route :brasileirao only to Brasileirao_Matches.csv (seasons 2012-2022); historico (2003-2019) is only consulted by get-standings' pre-2012 heuristic (tools.clj:248). A 2005 query with competition=brasileirao returns nothing from search_matches/get_team_stats/get_head_to_head, though the default (all) source includes historico.
suggestion: Merge historico into the :brasileirao source (or fold it in like get-standings does) so the competition filter spans all Brasileirao seasons.

title: get_standings produces league-style tables for knockout competitions
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools.clj:246 get-standings accepts copa-brasil/libertadores and runs build-standings (points = 3W+1D), which is not meaningful for single-elimination cups.
suggestion: Restrict get_standings to league competitions or label cup output as a points summary rather than standings.

title: Tool results returned as preformatted text rather than structured JSON
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/tools.clj:67-83 search-matches returns a human-formatted string; core.clj:36 wraps it in an MCP text content block.
suggestion: Matches the spec's example answer format; consider also returning structured fields for programmatic consumers.
